channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Clawdbot/Moltbot/OpenClaw is cool, but it gets pricey fast
item.description: Rarely have I more appreciated the chasm between me and Silicon Valley than I have while using OpenClaw. This new AI program, which previously went by Moltbot and before that Clawdbot, has achieved virality over the past week for its ability to control your digital life via text message.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: Startup Amutable plotting Linux security overhaul to counter hacking threats
item.description: If there’s one thing guaranteed to grab attention in the computer security world, it’s announcing yourself without fully explaining what it is you plan to do.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: NASA delays the first Artemis moonshot with astronauts because of extreme cold at the launch site
item.description: NASA has delayed astronauts' upcoming trip to the moon because of near-freezing temperatures expected at the launch site.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The best free cloud computing courses in 2026
item.description: The cloud isn’t new, but the way enterprises use it keeps evolving. AI workloads, new platform services, and tighter governance mean teams can’t rely on outdated knowledge. The real issue isn’t ignorance but drift.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: SpaceX and xAI could be merging. Why Elon Musk is doing it—and what might happen next
item.description: Two of Elon Musk’s best-known companies look likely to be headed for a megamerger ahead of a mooted IPO.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Who profits from AI? Not OpenAI, says think tank
item.description: Findings from a new study by Epoch AI, a non-profit research institute, appear to poke major holes in the notion that AI firms, and specifically OpenAI, will eventually become profitable.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Amazon in talks to invest up to $50 billion in OpenAI, WSJ says
item.description: Amazon.com Inc. is in talks to invest as much as $50 billion in OpenAI, the Wall Street Journal reported on Thursday, a deal that would accelerate ties between the world's largest cloud-computing provider and the leading builder of AI tools.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Humanoid robot makes architectural history by designing a building
item.description: Ai-Da Robot became the first humanoid robot to design a building with a modular Space Pod concept for Moon and Mars bases, featured at the Utzon Center in Denmark.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Apple touts ‘unparalleled’ protection for M5 Macs
item.description: Apple overnight updated the Apple Platform Security guide, its Bible for everyone involved in Apple security. The new edition confirms that M5 Macs now benefit from rock solid protection that should protect them against some of the most sophisticated attacks.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Chrome auto browse can help with work, says Google
item.description: Google is looking to expand Chrome’s role in enterprise productivity with a new auto-browse feature built on its Gemini 3 model that it says can navigate websites, gather information, and process it, reducing manual data entry and repetitive clicks in professional workflows.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Big Tech CEOs’ tortured statements on ICE in Minnesota show an industry captured by Trump
item.description: On the morning of January 24 in downtown Minneapolis, an ICE agent shot and killed protester Alex Pretti, an ICU nurse at a local Veterans Affairs hospital. Just 2 miles away, on January 7, another ICE agent had shot and killed Nicole Renee Good, a mother.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft 365: A guide to the updates
item.description: Microsoft 365 (and Office 365) subscribers get more frequent software updates than those who have purchased Office without a subscription, which means subscribers have access to the latest features, security patches, and bug fixes.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Nuclear safety rules quietly rewritten to favor AI
item.description: The US Department of Energy (DoE) program that would see three new experimental commercial nuclear reactors launched by July 4 is the driver behind the secret rewriting of nuclear safety and security standards, says a report released Wednesday by NPR.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Mplify launches AI-focused Carrier Ethernet certifications
item.description: The Carrier Ethernet market is undergoing a significant shift as AI workloads demand new performance validation from network infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 11 cool things Copilot can do in Excel
item.description: Microsoft has woven its generative AI technology throughout Microsoft 365, the company’s productivity suite. Its Copilot AI assistant is most often used in M365 apps for text-oriented actions, such as generating email or document drafts in Outlook or Word, or writing summaries of Teams meetings.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: To fix federal HR, Washington must stop rewarding failure
item.description: This is the "maintenance trap" in action: Agencies spend so much time and money keeping broken systems running that they can't afford to replace them.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network
